listings.title: Outlaw Audio RR 2160 Stereo Receiver - Like New
listings.body: Bought because I thought my other receiver's right channel was out but it was actually one of my interconnects that went bad.  It was only used < 3 hours so is in like new condition.  Smoke-free home. Comes in original box and accessories. No low ballers, please.  I'm in no rush to sell. Class A/B design (usually found in more expensive amps) with 110 watts for 8 ohms. 28 lbs. Outlaw Audio sells products directly to consumers so there's not that usual dealer markup.  Company and RR2160 have received high praise for outstanding sound and high quality/audiophile grade components at a moderate price. https://www.stereophile.com/content/outlaw-audio-rr2160-stereo-receiver  "...a conspicuously good-sounding audiophile product at a ridiculously low price. Highly recommended." https://www.soundandvision.com/content/outlaw-rr2160-stereo-receiver-review "...a stupendously great-sounding stereo receiver." https://www.cnet.com/news/outlaw-audio-rr-2160-the-audiophile-choice-for-stereo-receivers/ https://hometheaterhifi.com/reviews/receiver-processor/receivers/outlaw-audio-rr2160-stereo-retro-receiver-review/ I'm only free to meet curbside in NYC (Manhattan's Inwood, Eastside, Downtown Manhattan/Brooklyn, Navy Yard, Ft. Greene, Clinton Hill, North Williamsburg, Greenpoint, LIC) or along the Sprain/Taconic on some Sunday (sometimes Saturday) mornings. I would need to know by Friday afternoon. Meetup in Chinatown/LES preferred when in NYC. If interested, let me know when and where you would like to meet and please include your phone # when responding. Cash (only) and carry. No trades. All sales final.

listings.title: Pangea Power Cable/Cord
listings.body: Selling my Pangea powers cords as I don't listen to music as much.  These are the originals and improves the overall sound. The AC9 cords are actually 7 gauge; thick as a 3/4" garden hose. "You Don’t Always Have to Pay a Lot to Get a Lot" "The AC-9 is a perfect example of the adage that you don’t always have to pay a lot to get a lot, notes Sean Fowler in his April 13, 2010 review of the original-model AC-9 for SonicFlare.com. "As I listen to the AC-9, I struggle to think of a time when I heard any other affordable power cord deliver such a natural presentation without committing some kind of damning caveat." Everything was gently used or new/unused.  I am the original owner and not entertaining low ball offers even for multiple pieces; esp. if I have to drive to an outer borough as it adds significant travel time to my drive home. AC14 2.0 meters - SOLD AC14 C7 (meant for source components such as TV's, soundbars, DVD players...etc.) 3.0 meters - $100 (new in box) AC9 1.5 meters - $125 AC9 2.0 meters - $175 AC9SE 2.0 meters $225 I also have a 1.5 meter and 2.0 meter Pangea 24PC 4% sliver plated HDMI cables that are barely used; $20 or $25 respectively. Please note that I live in northern Westchester so am only free to meet in Manhattan's Inwood, Eastside, Downtown Manhattan/Brooklyn, Navy Yard, Ft. Greene,  Clinton Hill,  North Williamsburg, Greenpoint, LIC or along the Sprain/Taconic on Sunday (sometimes Saturday) mornings. I would need to know by Friday afternoon.  Preference for pickup in Inwood or Chinatown/LES when in NYC. When replying, let me know when, where you can meet along with your contact # to coordinate pickup. In a highly crowded market of bookshelf speakers at this price point, these are a standout choice.
